restriction: Bottled water allowed. No other food or drink.
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - Reading Room
description:
The Main Reading Room of the University Library is very quiet, spacious and constantly staffed. The room has a very high ceiling and is low-lit. There are rows of workspaces across the room, each with overhead table lights. It could be considered one of the more intense study spaces and is where the University Library's Research and Information Services Department is located. Staff are always on hand to answer any questions. We can offer specialist advice on the reference collections and on starting your research.  We can also help with ordering non-borrowable items from the UL's post-1950 modern collections, which can be collected from our circulation desk. There are five computer terminals for accessing items available through Electronic Legal Deposit and a CD-ROM computer if you need to access audio-visual material.

We have a range of assistive equipment available for readers, including ergonomic supports, page overlays, book supports and much more. Feel free to take whatever you need from 'dwarf shelf A' and return to the Reading Room at the end of your visit.

Please note that due to work on the West Room roof, there is currently noise from the building site. Staff can offer ear plugs and there are noise cancelling headphones available for your use.

title: Faculty of Architecture and History of Art – Ground Floor: Architecture and History of Art Room
description:
The library is located in the Faculty of Architecture and History of Art Building on the ground and basement floor. There is a variety of seating and study areas and the room has plenty of natural light coming from windows overlooking Trumpington Street. Step-free access is available to the ground floor of the library by lift from the central faculty corridor. If you have any accessibility needs, please let the library staff know and we will be happy to help.

Please check information on the [AHA LibGuide](https://libguides.cam.ac.uk/architecture-art) so you can plan your visit.

Step-free access to the ground floor and most of the basement is available by lift from the central faculty corridor. If you have any accessibility needs, please let the library staff know and we will be happy to help. Library staff are happy to fetch any items for library users. Please check information on the [AHA LibGuide](https://libguides.cam.ac.uk/architecture-art) so you can plan your visit.

description: We can be tricky to find, so take a look at our 'Finding us page' (<https://www.phil.cam.ac.uk/library/finding-us>) before your first visit.  In our small, but cosy, reading room you'll find large shared desks, four individual study spaces, a height-adjustable desk (with a screen, keyboard and mouse to connect your own device to), bean bags and cushions, and an iDiscover terminal. This area may suit those who prefer working in a calm and quiet space. Visit our website (see link below) for more information about our services, facilities and current opening hours (these can vary, especially during the vacations).

restriction: You must be a registered with the library to study at the UL
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - North Front Corridor
description:
The North Front Corridor is a bright wide space on the first floor landing where readers and staff will often walk by. It's a cooler area with easy access to both the North Front and the North Wing. There are large tables to work at and views out with natural light coming in from the North Courtyard. There are no plug sockets on the tables, though electrical outlets are not far away on the wall opposite. You are free to work in the enclosure by the Tea Room and Map Room. The enclosure has two computers available for use and is a cosy study space. You can also access the North Courtyard, where food and drink can be taken and the Tea Room is not far away for those all important snack breaks.

There are often engaging exhibitions in the North Front and South Front corridors. The current exhibition is 'Footlights Illuminated', based on the university's famous comedy club. Find more information on the Footlights Illuminated exhibition' webpage (https://www.lib.cam.ac.uk/footlights).

There is also a small sofa in this area. Located next to the library desk, this area may suit those who prefer working with some background noise. Visit our website (see link below) for more information about our services, facilities and current opening hours (these can vary, especially during the vacations).

Downing Place,
Cambridge

title: Language Centre: The John Trim Centre (JTC)
description:
The JTC facilities are specifically designed for language learning and offer various study spaces for flexible use for individual or group study.
Some workstations are equipped with DVD drives for multimedia playback of interactive learning resources, films and documentaries.
Borrowable DVDs, portable DVD players, and an Apple disc drive for Mac users are available.
The two supervision rooms have ample space for group activities, including the option to book computer monitor slots.
Reception can assist with booking various language learning activities.

The Fourth Floor Landing provides a very quiet space with a very small level of footfall. There is a large table with eight chairs, so this would be a good place to work with friends. This space can be accessed via the Stone Staircase from the first-floor landing. If step free access is required, there is a staff operated lift that can be taken to this space, please enquire at the Reader Services desk on the ground floor.

If you would like to find out more about what the University Library has to offer, see Our Services at a glance' webpage'.

description:
The lower ground area offers a range of mixed seating. You will find four and two seater study pods, study desks similar to what you would find elewhere in the building and a large space (the Glass room) with a high concentration of seating which is usually a quiet working area.

There are also two bookable study rooms. 

Overall this space is a quiet study area.

restriction: You need to be a registered reader at the University Library to use this space.
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - South Reading Room
description:
The South Reading Room is a very comfortable and cosy space with plenty of soft sofas and chairs. This is a quiet space with access to three acoustic booths and coffee tables. Two of the booths can be booked online on the LibCal calendar whilst the other booth operates on a walk-in basis.  (https://calendars.libraries.cam.ac.uk/calendar/acousticbooths?cid=8562&t=d&d=0000-00-00&cal=8562&inc=0).  

The toilets are just around the corner and you are within equal distance to the Main Reading Room and the Readers Services Desk; both of which are constantly staffed. The help desk is just outside on the first-floor landing. There is a printer/scanner at the end of the Reading Room South Corridor. You will often find book displays in this room, which showcase our wide variety of legal deposit material.  

The Library also offers zero-contact services such as : Click & Collect and Scan & Deliver. You can find more information on these services through our Zero Contact Services webpage. Follow the website link at the bottom of this listing for more details and updates

restriction: You must be a registered reader at the UL to use this space
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - North Reading Room
description:
The North Reading Room is a spacious room with multiple computers, iDiscover terminals and plenty of desk space for laptops. There are also three height adjustable desks. There are printers and scanners nearby in the Reading Room South corridor, the help desk is right next door on the 1st floor landing, and you are in equal distance to both the Main Reading Room and the Readers Services Desk.
For more information on library services, such as Click & Collect and Scan & Deliver, see our Zero-Contact services webpages. Follow the website link below for service updates.

restriction: You must be a registered reader at the UL to access this space.
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - South Front 5
description:
A well-lit space in a very quiet part of the University Library. Plug sockets are available, there is a good view out of the window and the air is cooler. The tables are small, with one or two chairs, and there are rarely many people around, idealisolated focus time. There are study spaces like this throughout the open book shelves. To reach this space, follow signs to the South Staircase and take the stairs/lift to the fifth floor. There is lift access available, however the space between the tables and the book shelves is extremely narrow.

If you would like to find out more about what the University Library has to offer, see 'Our Services at a glance' webpage.

restriction: The Rare Books room is for those who would like to consult items in the Rare Books collections.
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - Rare Books Room
description:
The Rare Books Room is a quiet and comfortable space on the first floor and warmer than some others. The reading room is for those who would like to consult the UL’s secondary collections of items printed before 1950 and other printed material that is rare or needs careful handling. iDiscover will direct you to the Rare Books Room if your material needs to be consulted in this location; books are fetched on request.

Most of this material can be ordered online through iDiscover or requested in person in the Rare Books room. You can find instructions for ordering Rare Books items on iDiscover on the Requesting Rare Books online webpage (https://www.lib.cam.ac.uk/collections/departments/rare-books/rare-books-services/requesting-rare-books-online).

This is a pencil only reading room and coats, bags, food and drinks are not permitted. There are plug sockets for laptops at all the desks. You will be close to the Tea Room and there is a disabled toilet just outside the Rare Books room.

When the Rare Books room itself is closed, services relocate to the Manuscripts Reading Room, including 09:30-16:30 on Saturdays.

restriction: You will need to be a registered reader at the University Library to enter this space.
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - Manuscripts Reading Room
description:
The Manuscripts Reading Room is open to readers for the consultation of the UL’s archive and manuscript collections with material available on request. It’s not necessary to make an appointment but it is helpful to contact the manuscripts team in advance to let them know when you want to come and what you want to see. Staff are on hand to help with ordering items and enquiries. Only pencils and laptop computers may be used in the Reading Room. All readers to the Manuscripts Reading Room are required to register when entering the room, and must leave prohibited items in the lockers provided.

You can find details of what is available in the UL's archival collections by using ArchiveSearch.

More details, including material fetching times and handling guidelines, are available on: Visiting the Manuscripts Reading Room.

restriction: You will need to be a registered reader at the UL to access this space.
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - Anderson Music Room
description:
The Anderson Room is open to everyone for the consultation of the UL’s collections of printed music and books about music printed after 1900. Earlier materials will be fetched to Rare Books or Manuscripts as appropriate. There are wide tables for the consultation of materials and an electronic piano available to play through scores.

For more information on the collections, opening hours and fetching times, see the Music Department webpages. Staff are on hand to assist between 9-5:10pm Monday to Thursday and 9-4:50pm on Fridays. The Anderson Room office is closed on Saturdays.

restriction: You will need to be a registered reader at the UL to use this space.
imagealt: UL - Aoi East Asian Reading Room
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - Aoi East Asian Reading Room
description:
The East Asian Reading Room (Aoi Pavilion) is a warm and welcoming study space, very quiet and provides a perfect environment for those wishing to study in a relaxed, warm and silent place. There are computers available for browsing the UL's online resources.The reference books in Chinese, Japanese and Korean are located in the East Asian Reading Room and users are welcome to use them when onsite.

In addition to the East Asian Reading Room on the first floor, the Aoi Pavilion contains the Library's collections of Chinese, Japanese and Korean material in two bookstacks on the Ground Floor (open stacks) and Basement (close stacks), together with two Seminar Rooms, and two offices for the staff of the Library's Chinese Section and Japanese and Korean Section. You can consult subjects experts on Chinese, Japanese and Korean language materials.

This space is staffed from 9am to 5:10pm Monday to Thursday, 9am to 16:50pm on Fridays and is not staffed (but open) on Saturday. For more information on opening hours and fetching times, please see the East Asian Reading Room webpage.

address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - Reading Room South Corridor
description:
The Reading Room South Corridor is a quiet corridor located next to the south courtyard.
The supplementary catalogue is located here and staff can be found in the Reading Room right next door.
There is a networked printer available for printing, scanning and copying.
The Library also offers Click & Collect, Scan & Deliver and Virtual Reading Room services.
For more details on these services please go to Zero Contact Services (https://www.lib.cam.ac.uk/using-library/zero-contact-services

address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - North Wing Corridor
description:
<!--StartFragment-->

The North Wing Corridor is a cooler space near the open stacks and has several large desks available. Printers and scanners are nearby, with the closest by the Rare Books Reading Room, and you are very close to the tearoom, toilets and the north courtyard.

The Library also offers Click & Collect, Scan & Deliver and Virtual Reading Room services.

Follow the Website link at the bottom of this listing for more details and updates. 

https://www.lib.cam.ac.uk/using-library/zero-contact-services

<!--EndFragment-->

restriction: Library is open Saturdays, out of term, except during the Summer vacation.
address: Wilberforce Road, Cambridge CB3 0WD
title: Moore Library - Ground Floor
description:
The Betty and Gordon Moore Library (known as the Moore) is a STEMM library with a strong collections emphasis towards Mathematics and the Physical Sciences. The Moore also has good collections across other STEMM collections. We also have excellent sections in literature, research and study skills and wellbeing. 

The ground floor area is the principle staff work area and where you will find the service desk. There are also large round tables that can be used for group study, University managed desktop PCs, self-service issue/return kiosk, E-legal deposit PC, an acoustic booth (which can be booked through the website) and the public printer/scanner.

address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - South Wing Corridor
description: The Library also offers: Book a Visit (to consult collections), Click & Collect, Scan & Deliver, Ask a Curator (special collections expertise). Follow the Website link at the bottom of this listing for more details and updates.

address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - South Front Corridor
description: The South Front Corridor is a bright wide space in an open shelving area where readers and staff will often walk by. It's a cooler area with easy access to both South Front and South Wing. There are no plug sockets on the tables, though electrical outlets are not far away.

address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - Map Room
description:
The Map Department is home to the majority of Cambridge University Library's cartographic resources, from early printed and manuscript maps through to modern resources including Ordnance Survey digital map data.
The Room has specially designed tables and benches for viewing the collections largest items. It also houses the card catalogue and Ordnance Survey Map Data PC. 
Staff are always on hand to help access the collection for both first time and experienced users. Readers may use laptops and other connected devices in the Map Room at any desk. Plug sockets are provided in all the desks and benches.
It is possible to make arrangements, on application to a member of the staff in the Map Room at least 20 minutes prior to the time of closing, for the transfer of most material to the Munby Rare Books Room for use there after the Map Room closes.
Please note that it may not be possible to consult some of the largest items in the collection outside of the Map Room due to their size.

OPENING TIMES
Monday to Thursday : 9am to 12:45pm and 2pm to 5:10pm
Friday : 9am to 12:45pm and 2pm to 4:50pm

restriction: For information on how to access the UL, please see: http://www.lib.cam.ac.uk/plan-your-visit/admissions/who-can-use
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - Tea Room
description: The UL Tea Room is a great place to meet up with friends and take a break from work. The atmosphere is chatty and social. The Tea Room is also suitable for taking phone calls/meetings. You can treat yourself to barista coffee, and a daily-changing variety of sandwiches, paninis, snacks and cakes. Serving hours are 9am to 3pm. You are also welcome to eat your own food in the Tea Room. Users without their own water bottle can ask for a paper cup from the Reader Services Desk in the Entrance Hall, in order to use the cooler.

restriction: Access is through a controlled reception area. The reception is staffed 9am-12:30pm & 1:30pm-5pm on weekdays, department members with card access can visit outside of these hours.

address: The University Centre, Granta Place, Cambridge, CB2 1RU
title: SU Lounge
description: Reception is open 9am-5pm Monday-Friday in and out of term time. Study space available 8am-7pm Monday-Friday.

imagealt: Cambridge Centre for Christianity Worldwide (CCCW) Library
address:
Cambridge Centre for Christianity Worldwide
Westminster College
Madingley Road
Cambridge
CB3 0AA
title: Cambridge Centre for Christianity Worldwide (CCCW) Library
description:
The library is open to all members of the public during our [regular opening hours](https://www.cccw.cam.ac.uk/about-us/visit-us/). The library is a welcoming place to browse and read, and there are study carrels for those who wish to work in the library. We have four small carrels with lights, usb points and plugs, and one fairly large desk, and additional soft seating.

Members of Cambridge University and the Cambridge Theological Federation are permitted to borrow up to ten books after a simple registration process. Registration is free. Bring along your University Library card.

External researchers and members of the public may also now join the library for free. An ID is required for external registration. Bring along a University Library card, if you have one. If not, the registration will take a bit longer and you will be given a CCCW library card for CCCW use only.

During July and August we are open mornings only 9:00-1300 and seasonal closures apply - Good Friday, Easter Monday, Christmas and New Year.

imagealt: UL - South Wing 3 Study Hub - Commonwealth Room
address: West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - South Wing 3 Study Hub - Commonwealth Room
description: This reading room is light and spacious, providing a quiet space to study with few distractions. It houses a good number of public computers and has a copier/printer. There are large desks and some smaller reading nooks available.

imagealt: UL - South Wing 3 Study Hub - Group Study Room
address:
Cambridge University Library,
West Road,
Cambridge,
CB3 9DR
title: UL - South Wing 3 Study Hub - Group Study Room
description:
The Group Study Room is a new bookable space for groups to meet and discuss their studies. It is located on the third floor, signposted from the nearby Beta staircase.

Online bookings can be made for up to 8 people for a session lasting 90 minutes. Only one person per group needs to make the booking. 

Each booking runs for 90 minutes during standard opening hours Monday-Saturday, with a 30 minute break between booked sessions.

At present, only one group can book to use the space for each available session. There are two tables available, each with five chairs.

We have actively risk assessed all spaces and space usage in accordance with University guidance on Covid-19, and have further safety measures in place including high quality mechanical ventilation in most areas of the building and a CO2 monitoring program.

We have deliberately factored in 30 minute breaks between each booking, to give a clear break between occupation - you are allowed to open the windows in the room but please remember to close them before you vacate at the end of your booking.

Our Student Experience Team will be in touch after your booking with a short survey to ask you about your experience booking and using this room.

restriction: Library is open Saturdays, out of term, except during the Summer vacation.
address: Wilberforce Road, Cambridge CB3 0WD
title: Moore Library - 2nd Floor
description:
The 2nd floor is at the top of the building and whilst, generally, a quiet space to work - can benoisy for some due to the large windows that automatically open, which are in the roof section of the building. This allows the wind to blow through which some people don't like - though others might find it the kind of background noise that aids concentration. 

Seating is arranged around the outer edge of the floor and offers nice views across the site and beyond.

address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - South Wing 3 Study Hub
description:
The South Wing 3 Study Hub is a suite of rooms that allow you to find the study space that suits you. Comprised of; the group study room, individual accessibility bookable study room, individual bookable study room for online zoom calls/supervisions etc, Commonwealth Reading Room (no booking required for this space), and bookable desks and lockers for PhD students in the Commonwealth Room, we hope you can find a space that is right for you. 

For more information on the options for using the Study Hub please look at the separate entries for the areas.

imagealt: UL - South Wing 3 Study Hub - Individual Accessibility Bookable Study Room
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - South Wing 3 Study Hub - Individual Accessibility Bookable Study Room
description: This is a bookable room, within the Group Study Room, equipped with height adjustable furniture.

imagealt: UL - South Wing 3 Study Hub - Individual Bookable Study Room
address: Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge, CB3 9DR
title: UL - South Wing 3 Study Hub - Individual Bookable Study Room
description: This is a bookable room, within the Group Study Room, perfect for online zoom calls, supervisions or lectures, as well as general study.

address:
West Hub, 
JJ Thomson Avenue
Cambridge, CB3 0US
title: West Hub
description:
West Hub is the University of Cambridge’s first publicly open co-working hub, providing flexible spaces in which to learn, socialise and collaborate.

We are open to everyone and feature a range of work and social spaces across three floors. We support group study / discussion or more individual, focused work. There is also a range of catering options and a shop with Post Office counter.

address: 184 Hills Road, Cambridge, CB2 8PQ
title: Education Library - Lower Ground Floor
description:
The Lower Ground Floor of the Library has some of the prettiest views in the Faculty. Study benches run the length of the windows overlooking the Faculty garden, where you can watch the squirrels, birds and sometimes ducks! 

The Library Living Room (also on the Lower Ground Floor) gives you an opportunity to rest, refresh and recharge.  Be creative with colouring books, ponder our puzzles or charge up your phone!   You will also find laptop stands (there are a few scattered around the Library too), desk lamps for extra light or blankets if the weather turns a bit chilly – the Library is Not Just a Roomful of Books!  

***For current Education Library opening hours, please email the Library team to check before your visit, particularly during vacations as times will change: [library@educ.cam.ac.uk](mailto:library@educ.cam.ac.uk)***

Follow the Website link at the bottom of this listing for more details and updates. You can also find the library on [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/edfaclib/).

address: 184 Hills Road, Cambridge, CB2 8PQ
title: Education Library - Lily Pad 1
description:
The Education faculty library has over a hundred study spaces. The upper levels of the Library (called Lily Pads) provide some cosy study areas, with some overlooking the front of the building.  **You’ll even come across a couple of study pods and the odd bean bag, too!** If you find it more comfortable to stand, then there are 3 standing desks available.

***For current Education Library opening hours, please email the Library team to check before your visit, particularly during vacations as times will change: [library@educ.cam.ac.uk](mailto:library@educ.cam.ac.uk)***

Follow the Website link at the bottom of this listing for more details and updates. You can also find the library on [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/edfaclib/).

description:
The SPRI library is open to all students, researchers and staff of the University of Cambridge, the wider polar research community and anybody wishing to know more about the polar regions. Follow the [Website link](https://www.spri.cam.ac.uk/library) for more details and updates.

The library is open 0900-1700 Monday to Friday but closes for lunch, between 1300-1400, each day.
